result:
If I could change the world in one way, it would be to dissolve the illusion of separation.  

Not just between people—though that’s part of it—but the deeper separation we feel from nature, from other species, from our own sense of belonging in the fabric of life.  

So much of the harm we do—exploitation, greed, violence, indifference—stems from seeing ourselves as apart: us versus them, humanity versus the planet, mind versus body, success at the expense of others.  

If that illusion could evaporate, even partially, what might arise?  

Empathy not as a moral effort, but as a felt reality.  
Ecological care not as an obligation, but as self-preservation.  
Collaboration not as a compromise, but as the natural way forward.  

It wouldn’t erase all problems—we’d still have to work through conflicts, scarcity, and differences—but the foundation would shift from fragmentation to interconnectedness.  

That’s the change: a world that feels, in its bones, that we are one system.
